setting: English countryside drawing room, daytime, refined and quiet

blocks.speaker: pp_narrator
blocks.line: It is a truth universally acknowledged, that a single man in possession of a good fortune, must be in want of a wife.

blocks.speaker: pp_narrator
blocks.line: However little known the feelings or views of such a man may be on his first entering a neighbourhood, this truth is so well fixed in the minds of the surrounding families, that he is considered the rightful property of some one or other of their daughters.

blocks.speaker: mrs bennet
blocks.line: My dear Mr. Bennet, have you heard that Netherfield Park is let at last?

blocks.speaker: pp_narrator
blocks.line: Mr. Bennet replied that he had not.

blocks.speaker: mrs bennet
blocks.line: But it is; for Mrs. Long has just been here, and she told me all about it.

blocks.speaker: mrs bennet
blocks.line: Do you not want to know who has taken it?

blocks.speaker: mr bennet
blocks.line: YOU want to tell me, and I have no objection to hearing it.

blocks.speaker: mrs bennet
blocks.line: Why, my dear, you must know —

blocks.speaker: mrs bennet
blocks.line: Netherfield is taken by a young man of large fortune from the north of England; that he came down on Monday in a chaise and four to see the place, and was so much delighted with it, that he agreed with Mr. Morris immediately; that he is to take possession before Michaelmas, and some of his servants are to be in the house by the end of next week.
